methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Hexana is a seed-stage nuclear energy company that has raised €21M to date under founder Gabriel Lévy. The valuation analysis draws from a nuclear energy peer universe of 11 companies: 3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private players including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private comparables, but filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage seed companies with similar valuation tiers yields fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical reliability. A peer-median relying on all nuclear energy companies regardless of stage would introduce material selection bias and obscure stage-specific risk.

Given the limited narrow peer set, triangulation via peer-median cannot proceed with confidence. Alternative methods—such as venture capital method calibration to sector-specific return expectations, revenue-multiple analysis if Hexana has disclosed pipeline metrics, or analogical pricing to the most proximate seed-to-Series A nuclear comparables—would require additional data not provided. The key uncertainty is the absence of stage-matched, tier-matched nuclear energy comps; this reflects both the sector's nascency and the heterogeneity of business models (modular reactors, fuel, licensing, etc.). Any valuation conclusion must remain conditional on these data gaps and should be revisited as the peer set matures.
